assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and email address.
No Vonage API account, Application, JWT, or channel choice exist yet.
No specific Vonage product goal has been supplied for this company-level roster entry.

developer_goal: Create a Vonage API account and reach the earliest official first-success outcome for one Vonage communications API.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved until one API and terminal are selected; candidate outcomes differ by Messages SMS, Voice, Verify, and other APIs.
first_success.observable_signal: Product-specific; for Messages SMS a successful POST returns an accepted response with message_uuid, but that terminal is not yet selected as the corpus default.

route_selection.surface: Vonage API developer documentation after Dashboard account signup
route_selection.rule: Lexicographic selection only after the API and first-success terminal are fixed; do not invent a winner among peer APIs.

candidates.name: Messages API SMS getting started
candidates.reason: Getting-started overview ends by telling developers to view documentation for their chosen API; Messages SMS is one complete candidate, not a labeled platform default.

candidates.name: Voice API first call or answer flow
candidates.reason: Voice is a peer API with Application and webhook requirements that differ from Messaging.

candidates.name: Verify or other Vonage API quickstarts
candidates.reason: The platform overview treats APIs as chosen destinations rather than ranking one universal first success.

required_path.action: Sign up for a Vonage API account and open the Developer Dashboard.

required_fields.label: Vonage account signup fields
required_fields.notes: Docs state signup yields an API key and secret; the public sign-up UI did not expose a stable field inventory to unauthenticated inspection.

required_path.observable_result: The Developer Dashboard shows an API key and secret and account balance controls.

required_path.action: Choose the Vonage API and first-success route to measure.

required_fields.label: API / channel choice
required_fields.notes: Official overview instructs developers to open documentation for their chosen API after shared fundamentals.

required_path.observable_result: A single API quickstart becomes the measured path.

prerequisites.description: A Vonage API account is required before Dashboard credentials and Applications are available.

platform_outcomes.description: Account signup creates an API key and secret and exposes balance management with initial free credit.

excluded.reason: The overview presents the CLI as an optional alternative to Dashboard tasks.

excluded.item: Reading documentation as a counted action
excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product interaction.

sources.title: Working with Vonage APIs
evidence_supported: Account signup for API key and secret
Instruction to continue in the chosen API's documentation

sources.title: Getting started with SMS on Messages API
evidence_supported: One complete Messages SMS candidate route after account fundamentals
Application and request-shaped terminal for SMS

uncertainties.question: Which Vonage API and terminal outcome should represent the platform for this roster entry?
uncertainties.impact: Messages, Voice, Verify, and other APIs change Applications, webhooks, auth mode, and success signals, so counts cannot be finalized.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus-level product goal, or a vendor-labeled default first API on the getting-started surface.

uncertainties.question: What exact current fields appear on Vonage API account signup?
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated walkthrough of Dashboard sign-up through first visibility of API key and secret.
